rationale: From the release-readiness lens the core is sound: main is CI-green (last 8 runs success, HEAD fa3e7f40), the self-revert is real (committed JSONL has mindspec-3d3i 'open'; Dolt has it 'closed'; post-merge re-imports JSONL), so chore-PR (a) durably fixes it — once committed JSONL == Dolt, the re-import is a no-op. v0.10.0 is correct: project is pre-1.0, flatten is opt-in/non-breaking via the flat→canonical→legacy resolver; shipping a brand-new mover with two open P2 bugs argues AGAINST signaling 1.0 stability. Pipeline is mature: tag-triggered, cosign keyless sigs, checksums.txt, syft SBOM, SHA-pinned actions, dryrun + install-smoke gates. TWO release-readiness gaps the plan gets wrong: (1) it promises release notes 'headlining' the flatten but provides NO mechanism — .goreleaser changelog only excludes docs/test/chore, so auto-notes will bury the flatten under 'Merge pull request'/'Merge bead' subjects; release.yml has no --release-notes/draft. (2) ADR-0039 is still 'Status: Proposed' and the flip to Accepted is bundled into the OPTIONAL step (c), so the release could headline a layout governed by a Proposed ADR. Both are cheap, must-fix-before-tag.
concrete_changes_required: Add a curated release-notes step before tagging: write the GitHub release body (or pass GoReleaser `--release-notes notes.md` / set `release.header`) so the flatten headline actually appears; also add `^Merge` to the .goreleaser.yml changelog excludes so auto-notes are not buried under 'Merge pull request #N' / 'Merge bead/...' subjects (current excludes only cover docs/test/chore).

Make the ADR-0039 Proposed -> Accepted flip MANDATORY and decoupled from the optional sc0w/3jq7 fixes — the shipped layout must not be governed by a 'Proposed' ADR. Do it in step (a) or a dedicated pre-tag step, not only inside optional step (c).

findings.issue: Plan claims release notes will headline the flatten, but .goreleaser.yml changelog auto-generates from commit subjects with excludes limited to ^docs:/^test:/^chore: and release.yml runs `release --clean` with no --release-notes/header/draft. Result: noisy auto-notes (feat/fix/impl + 'Merge pull request'/'Merge bead' lines), no headline. Needs an explicit curated-notes step.

ADR-0039 is Status: Proposed on main; the flip to Accepted is only in optional step (c). If (c) is skipped, the release headlines a layout whose governing ADR is still Proposed. The flip should be mandatory and independent of the migrate-bug fixes.

v0.10.0 is correct (0.x minor, opt-in non-breaking flatten). No case for v1.0.0 — a new migrate-layout mover with two open P2 bugs should not signal layout stability. No major needed: nothing breaks for existing flat/canonical/legacy projects.

Non-breaking is justified for the common case via the three-tier resolver + permanent multi-prefix matchers, BUT DetectLayout's 'mixed' state is a HARD ERROR. A partially-migrated or hand-mixed downstream tree will hard-fail the CLI. This is a deliberate guard, not regression of pre-existing projects, but should be called out in the release notes / migration guidance as a sharp edge.

release.yml does not set draft/prerelease — GoReleaser publishes immediately. For a headlined release, draft-then-edit-then-publish is safer. Also recommend a post-tag manual smoke beyond install-smoke (which only guards installer checksum logic): download the tagged binary, run `mindspec init` to confirm born-flat dirs, and run `migrate layout` against a legacy fixture.

Chore-PR (a) is durable: post-merge `bd hooks run post-merge` re-imports committed JSONL into Dolt; once the committed JSONL matches Dolt (epic+6 beads closed), the re-import is a no-op, so the self-revert cannot recur. Filing lifecycle bug (b) and deferring is correct — it is a dev/state-hygiene bug, not a shipped-binary defect. sc0w/3jq7 (opt-in, P2) need not block; ship with a 'known limitations/supervised' note.
